title: Choose text vs keyword per field intent
description: Map fields for full-text search as text (analyzed) and fields for filtering, sorting, faceting, or exact match as keyword (not analyzed).
body: Use when defining field mappings. Do not type an identifier, status, or facet field as analyzed text (it gets tokenized and exact match/sort/aggregation break) and do not type prose as keyword (full-text relevance is lost). Actions: map free text as text for relevance, map codes/enums/IDs/facets as keyword, and use a multi-field (text + keyword sub-field) when both are needed. Validation signals include exact filters and aggregations working on keyword fields and relevance scoring on text fields. Risk boundary: the wrong field type silently disables filtering or search and requires a reindex to fix; decide type by how the field is queried, not its content.
